title: President

result_summary.name: TTT Power Transition 2026
result_summary.description: Canonical TTT template locked 2026-04-05 after Map+Units finalization. 20 countries, 10x20 global hex grid, 2 tactical theaters (eastern_ereb, mashriq), 345 default units.

result_summary.note: Active + embarked only. Reserves are not observable.

final_assessment:
## Military Posture Assessment - Q1 2026

Columbia maintains a globally distributed force structure with **66 total units** currently engaged in active conflict with Persia. Our forces are strategically positioned across three primary domains:

**Forward Combat Deployments:** We have significant forces engaged in the Mashriq theater, with 5 tactical air units and 1 air defense system positioned in hex (8,11) alongside allied Mirai forces. Our air assets include F-16/F-35 rotations from Poland, Ramstein/Spangdahlem operations, and Gulf-based Prince Sultan/Al Udeid squadrons, supported by forward Patriot systems.

**Naval Power Projection:** Our naval forces dominate multiple theaters with 2 carrier strike groups in the Arabian Sea (CVN Lincoln and Ford), amphibious ready groups in the Red Sea, 7th Fleet operations near Formosa, and 6th Fleet presence in the Mediterranean. This provides comprehensive sea control from the Persian Gulf to the Western Pacific.

**Strategic Deterrent:** Our nuclear triad remains robust with ICBM silos in CONUS (Minuteman III at Warren/Malmstrom and Minot), Atlantic SSBN patrols, and strategic bomber capabilities. We maintain tactical nuclear assets forward-deployed in Europe.

**Key Vulnerabilities:** Most concerning is Persia's concentrated defensive posture in hex (8,13) with 8 active units including hardened S-300/S-400 air defenses protecting their nuclear sites. Their forces are well-dispersed across central and southern Iran, complicating our strike options.

**Strategic Advantages:** We possess overwhelming naval superiority, advanced tactical air capabilities, and extensive alliance basing from Europe to the Pacific. Our 32 reserve units provide substantial surge capacity, while our forward positioning enables rapid response across all theaters.

Our force posture reflects a superpower engaged in active regional conflict while maintaining global deterrence responsibilities.

content_blocks.text: I'll assess Columbia's military posture by gathering the necessary intelligence data. Let me start with our forces and strategic context.
Now let me check who our adversaries are and assess their visible forces, particularly Persia since we're at war with them.
Let me also check a few key hexes to better understand the operational picture, particularly around our forward positions.
